comment.body: To answer your question in the other thread, Alma: in short, my situation is math academia. What you wrote was pretty true, about ego, curiosity, humility/arrogance and academicians etc.. (It's not unusual to encounter arrogant colleagues or those who fight over authorship/citations etc..) It was a good reminder that motivates me; to be honest, your work ethic/philosophy is on par with that of some of the best mentors/colleagues I have seen.

comment.body: By the way, on the EU-bashing posts on Twitter I have a perhaps contrarian take: regulation doesn't necessarily thwart innovation. Thinking about the EU regulating USB chargers, AAPL has been generating profits from this type of trivialities for some time, which is not exactly hardcore innovation... So in this case, regulation and innovation seem to be on the same side.
comment.raw_body: By the way, on the EU-bashing posts on Twitter I have a perhaps contrarian take: regulation doesn't necessarily thwart innovation. Thinking about the EU regulating USB chargers, AAPL has been generating profits from this type of trivialities for some time, which is not exactly hardcore innovation... So in this case, regulation and innovation seem to be on the same side.
